title: Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Bihar

overview.short: Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary (also spelled Gautam Buddha) is a wildlife sanctuary located in Gaya district of Bihar state and Koderma district of Jharkhand state in east-central
overview.description: Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary (also spelled Gautam Buddha) is a wildlife sanctuary located in Gaya district of Bihar state and Koderma district of Jharkhand state in east-central India. The refuge was established in 1976, and covers an area of 259 km2. Prior to becoming a wildlife refuge, the area was a private hunting reserve. The refuge covers portions of the Lower Gangetic Plains moist deciduous forests and Chota Nagpur dry deciduous forests ecoregions.

topPlaces.name: Koderma Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Koderma Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the northern part of Koderma (community development block) in the Koderma subdivision of the Koderma district in the state of Jharkhand, India.

topPlaces.name: Rajauli Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Rajauli Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Nawada district in the Indian state of Bihar. Established in 2019, it is spread across an area of 27.27 km2 (10.53 sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Kodarma
topPlaces.description: Kodarma (also spelled as Koderma) is a city and a notified area in the Koderma subdivision of the Koderma district in the Indian state of Jharkhand. It is also the administrative headquarter of Koderma district.

items.note: Koderma Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the northern part of Koderma (community development block) in the Koderma subdivision of the Koderma district in the state of Jharkhand, India.
Rajauli Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Nawada district in the Indian state of Bihar. Established in 2019, it is spread across an area of 27.27 km2 (10.53 sq mi).

Kodarma (also spelled as Koderma) is a city and a notified area in the Koderma subdivision of the Koderma district in the Indian state of Jharkhand. It is also the administrative headquarter of Koderma district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gaya International Airport (66 km) and the nearest railway station is Gaya Junction (63 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gautam Budha Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Koderma Wildlife Sanctuary, Rajauli Wildlife Sanctuary, Kodarma.
